##RRUFFID=R250080
##IDEAL CHEMISTRY=Zn(UO_2_)(SO_4_)_2_(H_2_O)_5_
##LOCALITY=Blue Lizard mine, San Juan County, Utah, USA
##OWNER=Natural History Museum of Los Angeles County
##SOURCE=Tony Kampf
##DESCRIPTION=Yellow to orange–yellow transparent blades in a secondary assemblage with bobcookite, coquimbite, halotrichite, libbyite, metavoltine, rhomboclase, römerite, tamarugite and voltaite. Type sample, described by Kampf et al (2023)
##STATUS=The identification of this mineral is confirmed by single-crystal X-ray diffraction and chemical analysis
##URL=https://www.rruff.net/odr/rruff_sample/R250080
##MEASURED CHEMISTRY=(Zn_0.675_Mg_0.102_Fe_0.085_Mn_0.043_Co_0.033_)(U_1.028_O_2_)(SO_4_)_2_(H_1.991_O)_5_ ; H2O, calculated based upon the structure determination (S = 2 and O = 15 atoms per formula unit)
##DIFFRACTION SAMPLE DESCRIPTION=Powder
